title: 'They start looking for your successor': Rohit Sharma faces blunt warning
content: Former England spinner Graeme Swann believes discussions around Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli's futures are natural.  He feels the upcoming England tour is crucial for Rohit.  Swann is impressed by young Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's talent.  He also shared surprise at Suryakumar Yadav's removal as T20I captain.  The focus remains on India's senior players and emerging stars.

title: Maharashtra man stabs women multiple times over personal dispute, chilling CCTV footage surfaces
content: A Palghar District Collector's Office employee, Snehal Sawant, was critically injured in a daylight stabbing near Gold Cinema. CCTV footage shows Anmol Mule allegedly attacking her multiple times with a sharp weapon.  Police suspect a personal dispute, possibly relationship-related, as the motive. Mule is currently absconding, and a search is ongoing.

title: Microsoft president Brad Smith has a message for students booing tech CEOs
content: Microsoft president Brad Smith has responded to graduating students who booed AI at commencement ceremonies this spring, calling the backlash a "powerful wake-up call for the tech sector." In a 3,000-word essay, Smith acknowledged the class of 2026 faces a "perfect storm" of AI automation and tech layoffs, yet urged graduates to adapt rather than fear AI. His optimistic message arrives as Sam Altman, Mustafa Suleyman, and Dario Amodei clash over AI's job impact.

title: 'Innovation in India's DNA': PM Modi, Macron inaugurate 'Bharat Innovates' in Nice
content: Prime Minister Modi and French President Macron inaugurated Bharat Innovates 2026 in Nice, fostering India-France tech and innovation cooperation. The event connects Indian startups and institutions with global investors. Leaders emphasized shared vision and India's growing role as a global innovation leader, highlighting achievements like Chandrayaan-3.

title: Iran to immediately open Hormuz, US to lift Naval blockade: Draft deal details emerge
content: A draft memorandum between Iran and the US reportedly includes Iran's commitment to not produce nuclear weapons and reopening the Strait of Hormuz. The US would lift its naval blockade and provide sanctions relief, including releasing frozen Iranian assets. Both sides are expected to finalize implementation details in the coming days.

title: Yogi’s UP beats national violent-crime decline across 5 key categories
content: NCRB data for 2022-24 shows UP outperformed the national trend in murder, rape, kidnapping, rioting and attempt to murder, though attempt to murder remains the state’s weakest decline

title: Canada's sixth-largest state Manitoba retires student pathway to permanent residency
content: Manitoba has closed its Career Employment Pathway for international graduates, effective immediately. Candidates with active EOIs and six months of provincial work experience can now be considered under the Skilled Worker in Manitoba Pathway. This change aims to align graduates' skills with labor market needs, prioritizing those who studied and are working in the province.

title: Maharashtra Home Department orders probe into social media content of Pranit More
content: The Maharashtra Home Department has ordered a probe into all videos of stand-up comedian Pranit More after his show sparked a major controversy. The Maharashtra Cyber Police registered an FIR against Pranit More, Himanshu Jangra and Sejal Pawar. Both Jangra and Pawar faced serious consequences, with Jangra losing his job and Pawar being put on compulsory leave.

title: Meta's highest paid employee Alexandr Wang 'admits' the company's previous AI policy didn't work
content: Meta's Chief AI Officer Alexandr Wang has admitted that the company's open-source AI playbook no longer fits its frontier models, with Muse Spark kept proprietary after early training flagged bio risk and other safety concerns. Wang says rival AI labs are seeing the same risks scale up. Meanwhile, Meta is testing subscriptions on Instagram, Facebook, WhatsApp and its AI chatbot to diversify beyond ads.

title: NFL rumors: 49ers target $10M Giants edge before camp
content: The San Francisco 49ers are reportedly expected to pursue an edge rusher before training camp, with New York Giants defender Kayvon Thibodeaux emerging as a realistic trade target. NFL insiders believe San Francisco wants to strengthen its pass rush after struggling defensively last season. While stars like Maxx Crosby seem financially unrealistic, Thibodeaux’s rookie contract makes him a more manageable option. Other names, including Arizona Cardinals pass rusher Josh Sweat, have also surfaced in trade discussions.

title: Quote of the day by Morgan Freeman
content: Renowned actor Morgan Freeman recently delivered a thought-provoking message about the importance of living in the now, drawing inspiration from contemporaries like Jim Carrey. He highlighted the significance of finding peace in stillness, encouraging individuals to allow life to flow organically instead of relentlessly seeking to dominate every situation.

title: UP man stabs 19-year-old daughter to death inside police station over love affair
content: Tragedy struck in Banda district as a 19-year-old woman was allegedly stabbed to death by her father inside a police station. Recovered by police after eloping, she was reunited with her family at Badausa station. During counseling, her father reportedly attacked her with a sharp weapon. She later succumbed to her injuries.

title: Arhery: Dhiraj, Kumkum topple Olympic champions to win gold in World Cup
content: Facing one of the strongest teams in international archery, Dhiraj and Kumkum stayed in control throughout the match and made the most of key moments.

title: Indian vessel sinks off Oman; rescue op on to save 14 crew members as US navy joins efforts
content: An Indian-flagged vessel, Virat 1, carrying 14 crew members, has been involved in an incident off Oman's coast. Search and rescue operations are actively under way, coordinated with Omani authorities and nearby vessels. This event highlights ongoing concerns for Indian seafarers in the region, following recent incidents involving fatalities and diplomatic discussions.

title: Surbhi Jyoti welcomes baby girl with husband Sumit Suri
content: Television actress Surbhi Jyoti and husband Sumit Suri welcomed their first child, a baby girl, on June 13, 2026. The couple shared the joyous news on Instagram, expressing their love and gratitude. This follows Surbhi's pregnancy announcement in February and her wedding to businessman Sumit Suri in October 2024.

title: Another Sena split? Uddhav calls emergency meeting of MPs amid defection buzz
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) asserted all nine MPs attended Uddhav Thackeray's meeting, with five joining virtually due to valid reasons like family illness or election duties. This gathering aimed to quell speculation of a significant party split, following recent meetings between some UBT MPs and leaders of the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena.

title: Sooryavanshi sends special message to Team India ahead of T20 WC clash vs Pakistan
content: Teen batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has thrown his support behind the Indian Women's T20 World Cup team ahead of their crucial match against Pakistan. The 15-year-old, who had a stellar IPL 2026, shared a heartfelt message on Instagram, boosting team morale. India, with a strong historical record against Pakistan, aims for a winning start in Birmingham.

title: 'Won't take body until encounter': Tension grips Dehradun after communal clash; what we know
content: Uttarakhand Chief Minister Pushkar Singh Dhami vowed "strictest possible action" against lawbreakers following a fatal clash in Dehradun. The violence, reportedly over a water dispute, left one dead and three injured. Dhami assured no one would escape justice, as police investigate and have begun demolishing an accused's property.

title: 'No disaster awaiting India': Sitharaman dismisses Rahul's 'economic tsunami' warnings
content: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman strongly defended the Modi government's achievements, countering Rahul Gandhi's criticisms. She highlighted India's robust economic growth amidst global challenges, citing IMF acknowledgments and significant poverty reduction. Sitharaman also detailed the government's welfare initiatives and infrastructure investments, particularly in Karnataka, urging collective effort towards a developed India by 2047.

title: Raushan Sir's brother, named in Khan Sir coaching vandalism case, dies in Nepal
content: The brother of coaching educator Raushan Anand, accused in the vandalism of Khan Sir's institute, has died under mysterious circumstances in Nepal. This development fuels speculation surrounding the high-profile case, which involves allegations of firing by security guards and a legal battle for Khan Sir's anticipatory bail.

title: ‘Will speak when time is right’: Saayoni Ghosh reaches Delhi amid TMC turmoil
content: TMC MP Saayoni Ghosh arrived in Delhi amid speculation of joining a rebel faction, declining to comment. This follows another TMC MP's arrival as a proposed meeting with the Lok Sabha Speaker looms. The party faces internal dissent, with a significant number of MPs and MLAs reportedly backing the dissident camp.

title: Govind Namdev accuses 'Kala Hiran' makers of misrepresenting Salman case
content: Veteran actor Govind Namdev has alleged that the makers of 'Kala Hiran' misled him about the film's story. He said he was shocked after watching the trailer and felt cheated. Meanwhile, Salman Khan's legal team has also sent a notice to the makers, demanding an immediate halt to the film's release.

title: 'Governor' box office collection Day 3 [LIVE]: Eyes Rs 3 crore opening weekend on first Sunday
content: Chinmay D. Mandlekar's 'Governor', starring Manoj Bajpayee as a fictionalised RBI Governor navigating the 1990 Indian economic crisis, is closing out its opening weekend with a cumulative domestic net of Rs 2.42 crore. After a Day 1 net of Rs 0.90 crore across 1,427 shows and a promising 27.8% jump to Rs 1.15 crore on Day 2. Produced by Vipul Amrutlal Shah under Sunshine Pictures and featuring music by Amit Trivedi with lyrics by Javed Akhtar, the UA 13+ certified financial thriller has arrived to critical interest but faces the familiar challenge of converting niche subject matter into mainstream footfall. Final Day 3 figures are still to be consolidated.

title: 'Bring the cup home': Indian men's team sends message before IND vs PAK clash
content: India's Women's T20 World Cup campaign receives a significant morale boost as male cricketers send messages of support. Rohit Sharma, Gautam Gambhir, and Shubman Gill have rallied behind the team, urging them to play fearlessly and bring home the coveted trophy. The Women in Blue are aiming for their maiden T20 World Cup title, starting their journey against Pakistan.

title: Suspecting infidelity, boyfriend slits Sikkim woman’s throat in Bengaluru
content: A 22-year-old woman from Sikkim, working as a receptionist in Bengaluru, was found murdered at her rented residence. Police allege her boyfriend, a hotel waiter from Darjeeling, killed her with a kitchen knife after suspecting her fidelity. The couple had recently moved to the city for work.

title: 'Discipline ...': Manu Bhaker's father recalls Jaspal Rana's biggest contribution
content: Manu Bhaker's father revealed legendary shooter Jaspal Rana's profound impact, instilling discipline, self-belief, and a result-oriented mindset in his daughter. Rana's unwavering support for Manu's dual-event ambition proved crucial in Paris. His guidance shaped her into a champion, emphasising hard work and confidence.

title: 'Main Vaapas Aaunga' BO day 3 [LIVE]: Crosses Rs 4 crore net milestone
content: Imtiaz Ali’s latest historical romantic drama 'Main Vaapas Aaunga', starring Diljit Dosanjh and Sharvari Wagh, has completed its first three days at the box office. Following a slow start on Day 1, the film witnessed a substantial 60.9% growth on Saturday. Discover the full breakdown of the movie's Day 3 box office collections, early trends, theatrical show counts, net earnings, and gross domestic totals inside.

title: Late-night ride in client’s Mercedes turns fatal for car dealer, friend in Bengaluru
content: A late-night drive in a Mercedes-Benz SUV tragically ended near Hoskote after crashing into a divider on the Satellite Town Ring Road. The driver, car dealer Rizwan, was found in a water body, while his companion, Saajiya, died at the scene. Police suspect speeding and reckless driving caused the fatal accident.

title: Reborn in the USA: Folarin Balogun and the joy of the global football
content: Folarin Balogun, scorer of two goals on his World Cup debut, was born in Brooklyn to Nigerian parents after his pregnant mother, visiting her sister in America, was reportedly advised against flying back to Britain, where she lived at that time. In the more extreme corners of the MAGA universe, that would make Balogun an "anchor baby" – born to someone visiting the US. On Thursday night, he was simply called "Man of the Match" the undisputed hero of a squad that perfectly represents the beautiful, messy, borderless reality of modern soccer.

title: UP govt forms 3-member SIT to investigate Ram temple donation case
content: Uttar Pradesh government has formed a three-member SIT to investigate alleged embezzlement of donations at the Ram Temple in Ayodhya. The move follows a request from the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, which seeks to counter misinformation. The SIT will submit a preliminary report within seven days.

title: 14 Indians Evacuated After Vessel Suffers Engine Failure Off Oman Coast
content: Reports suggested that the crew members abandoned the vessel and embarked on a life raft dropped by a maritime patrol vessel.

title: "India Is Country Of Innovation:" France's Macron At 'Bharat Innovates' Event
content: French President Emmanuel Macron on Sunday said India is a nation of innovation, adding that both countries have a true partnership in critical sectors like AI and climate change.

title: US-Iran War LIVE Updates: Tehran Says No Decision On US Deal, Qatar Delegation Arrives For Talks
content: US-Iran War LIVE Updates: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei reportedly denied that a memorandum of understanding would be signed.

title: Over 500 Indian Sailors Stranded In Strait Of Hormuz For 107 Days
content: Over 500 sailors have been stranded in the Strait of Hormuz and the Gulf of Oman since the onset of hostilities between the US and Iran

title: Riots Bring Belfast To Standstill. How Race-Hate Tension Triggered Protests
content: During the violence, shops were forced to close, events were cancelled, and public transport was badly affected.

title: How India Walked The Middle East Tightrope Without Blinking
content: India's West Asia balancing act does not exist in isolation; it sits inside a larger geometry of partnerships.

title: Raushan Sir's Brother, Accused Of Khan Sir Coaching Vandalism, Dies In Nepal
content: Raushan Sir and Khan Sir had been at the centre of a major rivalry between two coaching centres in Patna, Gyan Bindu GS Academy and Khan Global Studies.

title: Automatic Weapons, 13 Bullets: Daring Chandigarh Murder Caught On Camera
content: The attack took place at around 2:30 pm. Three attackers arrived at the shop on a motorcycle; two entered.

title: Deadly Philippine Quake Raised Seabed By Up To Two Metres
content: A powerful earthquake that killed at least 61 people in the Philippines this week raised the seabed by as much as two metres (6.6 feet), exposing coral and harming...

title: 1 Killed, House Set On Fire As Water Dispute In Uttarakhand Turns Violent
content: Heavy police deployment was seen in the village as of Sunday even as scores of people gathered to protest against the incident and demand justice.

title: German Heiress Shot Dead At Family's Luxury Safari Estate In South Africa
content: The heiress had reportedly recently purchased additional properties in the region and was managing wildlife-related operations at the lodge.

title: Inside 24-Hour Talks That Led White House To Act Against Anthropic's AI Model
content: The move came after concerns were raised about Anthropic's newly released AI model, Fable, which the company had said was safe.

title: First-Of-A-Kind New York Law Seeks Tech To Block 3D Printers From Making Guns
content: About one-third of US states already have taken steps to ban or regulate build-it-yourself firearms that lack serial numbers.

title: US Offers No Regret Over Indians Killed In Strike. Shashi Tharoor Hits Out
content: Shashoo Tharoor sought to know why non-lethal means are not used to stop non-compliant ships in the Gulf region.

title: Switzerland Votes On Proposal To Cap Population At 10 Million
content: Swiss voters decide on Sunday whether to back a proposal to cap the country's population in a referendum likened to Britain's Brexit vote,

title: High-school Dropout To Crime Boss: About Venezuela's 'Nino Guerrero' Killed In US Raid
content: The Tren de Aragua leader killed in a US-Venezuelan raid was a high school dropout who lived in comfort behind bars as he transformed a prison gang into one of the most powerful...

title: 2 Killed As Mercedes-Benz Loses Control, Crashes Into Divider in Bengaluru
content: Preliminary findings suggest that the car was being driven at high speed at the time of the accident. Police suspect over-speeding may have been a major factor behind the crash.

title: Eye surgeon couple from Mysuru lead camp in Ghana
content: Highlighting the urgent need for paediatric eye care in Sub Saharan Africa, Dr. K.V. Ravishankar an estimated 80,000 children require cataract surgery in the region

title: Census 2027: Thiruvananthapuram district to have 5,527 enumerators for Houselisting and Housing Census
content: First phase of Census will kick off with a self-enumeration phase on June 16; field visits by enumerators will be carried out from July 1 to July 30

title: Mob disrupts Railway traffic near Patna, alleges lack of adequate trains for competitive exam; police resort to lathi-charge
content: Top police offical says “more than 200 young men, posing as students appearing for competitive exams” turned up at the Pataliputra station

title: Revenue Bar Association challenges constitutional validity of various provisions of Finance Act, 2026
content: The 2026 law had introduced many new provisions to the Income Tax Act, 1961 with retrospective effect dating back as early as June 1, 2007

title: Mechanised sailing vessel experiences engine failure off Oman
content: Virat was sailing off the coast of Oman, reportedly with 14 Indian crew members; rescue operation is currently under way with ships in the vicinity

title: Visakhapatnam airport should stay as domestic airport after Bhogapuram, says BJP’s Vishnu Kumar Raju
content: P. Vishnu Kumar Raju said lakhs use the city airport and that retaining both would help north Andhra connectivity

title: CJP protest in Bengaluru LIVE: Prakash Raj joins protest; We are challenging everyone, including SC and CJI, says spokesperson
content: Cockroach Janta Party is holding a peaceful protest in Bengaluru today, as part of its nationwide campaign demanding the resignation of Union Minister Dharmendra Pradhan

title: PM’s Europe visit begins in Nice, with tech and bilateral ties in focus
content: In addition to Nice, this France visit includes programmes in Evian and Paris, with a possible bilateral meeting with U.S. President Trump

title: APSRTC to run 100 electric buses in Visakhapatnam, with focus on Bhogapuram airport link
content: The first 20 buses arrive in June, the rest by August, split between the Gajuwaka and Simhachalam depots

title: UDF endorses demand for bifurcation of Kerala’s Malappuram district
content: District-level meeting of the alliance discusses proposal, decides to seek  appointment of a special officer to examine its feasibility

title: Vice-Chancellors’ participation at RSS event draws sharp criticism in Kerala
content: KUHS V-C Mohanan Kunnummal, MG University V-C in-charge Mavoothu D. and Thunchath Ezhuthachan Malayalam University V-C in-charge C.R. Prasad were present at the event held in Thiruvananthapuram on June 13

title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay ‘shops’ AIADMK MLAs, alleges Stalin
content: The DMK President said democratic forces should also question what the MLAs had received in return for joining the TVK

title: Indian Army unveils new uniform policy, phases out colonial-era practices
content: The new policy reflects the Army’s effort to align its dress regulations with contemporary Indian values while maintaining the service’s rich traditions and professional standards

title: University of Agricultural Sciences-Bengaluru ranked 7th in country and first in Karnataka
content: The Indian Institutional Ranking Framework (IIRF) that has placed the UAS-B in 7th position was based on the University’s excellence in research, teaching, agri extension and industry collobaration among others

title: Tamil Maanila Congress exits NDA
content: Tamil Maanila Congress (Moopanar) leader G.K. Vasan made the announcement after chairing the party’s executive committee meeting.

title: India is innovating for a sustainable future; India is innovating for the world, says PM Modi
content: PM Modi was warmly welcomed by the French President in Nice, where the two leaders greeted each other with a hug

title: Interactive session on CET/COMEDK at JNNCE in Shivamogga
content: JNN College of Engineering Principal  spoke after inaugurating an interaction on CET/COMEDK organised for students aspiring to pursue engineering and their parents at the MBA-MCA auditorium of JNN Engineering College

title: Palaniswami says more effective use of social media yielded results for TVK in Tamil Nadu Assembly elections
content: AIADMK begins district-wise consultations with functionaries and senior leaders to review its performance in the Assembly elections

title: Lack of coordination in handling Nipah outbreak, alleges Leader of the Opposition Pinarayi Vijayan
content: The Health Minister and the Health department, who are supposed to take a leadership role in this, has not been coordinating well with the district administration, says LoP Pinarayi Vijayan

title: Kerala Nipah virus: Here’s what you need to know
content: Nipah virus has been reported multiple times in Kerala in the past. Strictly following the guidelines issued by health authorities is a must to curb the spread of misinformation

title: Three close contacts of Nipah-infected person in Kozhikode test negative for virus
content: Revenue Minister A.P. Anil Kumar, who is in charge of Kozhikode district, says there is no large-scale spread of the infection at present and no need to declare a containment zone

title: Political journey of Pinarayi Vijayan: a timeline of key milestones
content: Pinarayi Vijayan rose from a grassroots CPI(M) worker in Kannur to become one of Kerala’s most influential and longest-serving Left leaders.

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE: Cyclonic system to bring rains to northern Tamil Nadu
content: According to the Regional Meteorological Centre in Chennai, moderate rain accompanied by thunderstorm and lightning is likely to occur in northern districts of Chennai

title: Sonam Wangchuk joins Cockroach Janta Party’s protest in Hyderabad; seeks Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation
content: Posters reading “Dharmendra Pradhan Resign” competed for attention while volunteers and supporters slowly filled the tent erected at the venue

title: KSRTC free travel for women: Everything about Priyadarshini scheme
content: This program, part of the UDF government’s ‘Indira Guarantees,’ covers seven categories of KSRTC ordinary buses, allowing women passengers to obtain ‘zero’ fare tickets without the need for identification.

title: Fear of urea shortage in Nellore district is unfounded, says Kovur MLA Vemireddy Prashanthi Reddy
content: Vemireddy Prashanthi Reddy said the urea ration was raised to three bags an acre and blamed vested interests for shortage rumours

title: Minor among four apprehended for alleged role in Govindpuri fire that killed three
content: A 17-year-old girl from Navjeevan Camp, Govindpuri was identified and apprehended. During questioning, she allegedly disclosed that a 27-year-old woman, Sarita, allegedly instigated her to set ablaze a scooter parked in the building, according to police.

title: CBSE Class 9 students in Chennai yet to get textbooks for this year
content: Some schools have received their English and Science textbooks while most have not received their subject books; an NCERT book supplier says the situation will likely ease by next week

title: H.D. Kumaraswamy says Bidadi township is a real estate project
content: Bidadi township was not in the interest of the State, but will help land mafia and land grabbers’ profit. The Janata Dal (Secular) will stand with the farmers and seek legal remedy against the project, says Union Minister for Heavy Industries

title: 20 hurt as Bengaluru-bound APSRTC bus rams parked truck near Chintamani
content: The Udayagiri depot bus, carrying 40 passengers from Pamuru, struck the truck near Chintamani in the early hours of Sunday

title: Toddler missing: NDRF, SDRF teams deployed to take up search operations in forests
content: The toddler disappeared while playing in the garden in Ch. Agraharam, Tuni mandal in Kakinada district, on June 6. Police registered a ‘girl missing’ case and are trying to trace the girl

title: Constitutional expert questions plan of TMC rebel MPs to seek ‘real TMC’ status from Speaker
content: Former Lok Sabha secretary general and constitutional expert P.D.T. Achary  said that under the present circumstances in the TMC case, the Lok Sabha Speaker has no role

title: IUML, UDF in a sticky wicket on the nomination of non-Muslim members to Waqf Board
content: The onus is on the IUML and the UDF to take a call on the communally sensitive issue as a BJP leader has moved the Kerala High Court challenging the non-inclusion of non-Muslims in the Board

title: Pawan Kalyan to address Jana Sena’s national integration meeting in Delhi on July 15
content: The party said leaders who recently joined from four southern States would take part in the deliberations

title: T.N. Opposition leaders condemn reduction in Aavin Green Magic milk sales; government denies claim
content: During the DMK regime, a similar decision was made in 2023 to stop the sale of the green-packet milk and replace it with the 3.5% fat purple-packet milk. The DMK government dropped the plan back then following stiff opposition, recalled Annamalai.

title: NDA inches towards two-thirds majority in Rajya Sabha; short in Lok Sabha despite TMC rebellion
content: In the Lok Sabha, however, the NDA's numbers can go up to 213 with around 20 more TMC MPs forming a separate group and supporting it

title: Trinamool rebellion reaches Delhi: 20 party MPs plan to form a separate bloc and support NDA
content: 20 of the 29 Lok Sabha MPs want to create a separate bloc and support the BJP-led NDA government in Parliament; Barasat Lok Sabha MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar is emerging as the leader of the group; West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari and Union Minister Bhupendra Yadav in contact with the rebel camp

title: Constitution Amendment Bill, part of delimitation package, defeated
content: The Bill falls short of the two-thirds mark of 352 votes, with 298 in favour and 230 against in the House where 528 members were present at the time of voting

title: How is a Constitution Amendment Bill passed?
content: A Constitutional Amendment Bill must secure two-thirds of majority votes in both Houses, and get ratified by at least 50% State legislatures, if it affects the federal structure, before receiving an assent from President

title: $19 million air conditioners sale dispute: Madras High Court rejects plea against Fujitsu General
content: Judges reject ETA General’s plea to dismiss a civil suit filed by Fujitsu General (Thailand) for recovery of the money and instead refer the dispute for arbitration

title: TMC crisis continues: Saayoni, Mala Roy reach Delhi ahead of rebels' meeting with speaker
content: The rebel MPs are also likely to hold a meeting with West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari on Sunday, sources said.

title: Why Census 2027 matters for development, democracy and representation
content: Census 2027, the world’s largest count of people within a national geography, is an overdue exercise with far-reaching implications; an accurate and comprehensive count will ensure that no one is left behind and that policies, entitlements, and resources are guided by reliable data

title: Training of field workers for Census 2027 to begin in Chennai on June 15
content: The first phase of training for field trainers is expected to be conducted in Chennai Corporation offices at five locations from June 15 to June 17.

title: Where Indian seafarers’ safety is at stake
content: Seafarers operating in regions where commercial shipping is entangled in geopolitical conflict are at a greater risk; diplomatic engagement alone may not suffice, they should not be compelled to sail through war-risk zones without full disclosure of risks and their informed consent

title: 'Obedient servant, complies with orders: Rahul slams PM over U.S. statements
content: "A compromised PM will not defend the nation's honor because he is beholden to those who insult the country," the LoP said.

title: Rebel AIADMK leader C.Ve. Shanmugam accuses Edappadi Palaniswami of suppressing dissent
content: He said the party is falling into the clutches of a single family and accused Mr. Palaniswami of staging a “drama” to facilitate his son’s entry into the party. Mr. Shanmugam claimed that 31 of the 41 Assembly seats won by AIADMK in the 2026 Assembly elections were “alms” given by the PMK.

title: Stand-up comedians who cross limits need to be taught lesson: Sena MLC to bring motion in House
content: "A lesson should be taught to such stand-up comedians. They are crossing all limits and making fun of anyone. They say whatever they want. I will introduce a motion in the upcoming monsoon session against such acts," says Shiv Sena leader

title: Police hunt for gangs recruiting students into drug trade
content: As part of the initiative, SPGs functioning in more than 250  schools across the district will be mobilised to assist law enforcement agencies in gathering information, strengthening surveillance and identifying vulnerable students who may be drawn into drug distribution networks

title: Mamata rejigs TMC's youth, women wings; removes Saayoni, Mala Roy from key positions
content: TMC also replaced on Kolkata Dakshin MP Mala Roy with Alifa Ahmed, TMC MLA from Kaliganj in Nadia district, as the president of its women's wing, the Trinamool Mahila Congress, party sources said

title: CAG flags shortcomings at Social Audit Unit of Assam; suggests corrective measures
content: Gross shortfall in holding of governing body meetings implies the deficiency in monitoring at the government level, reads a CAG report in Social Audit Units in Assam

title: Actor Gautami Tadimalla quits AIADMK
content: In her resignation letter to AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K. Palaniswami, Ms. Tadimalla cited prevailing political circumstances and her desire to get involved in social service as reasons for her decision.

title: Indira Gandhi's historic address at 1st UN conference on environment a milestone: Jairam Ramesh
content: "Indira Gandhi's speech is widely considered to be one of the four milestones in the global discourse on the environment, the others being the publication of Rachel Carson's Silent Spring in 1962, Paul Ehrlich's The Population Bomb in 1968, and MIT/Club of Rome's The Limits to Growth in early 1972," says Jairam Ramesh

title: Nandyal MP Byreddy Shabari to distribute assistive devices to 3,000 beneficiaries on Monday
content: The devices, for differently-abled persons and senior citizens, were identified at camps held across the seven Assembly constituencies last year

title: Odisha govt eases registration of pre-RERA apartments: Who can avail the new relief?
content: The TOI Real Estate Desk is a focused team of seasoned journalists and market watchers dedicated to decoding the ever-evolving property landscape for The Times of India readers.
